Article Title: Pawan Khera seeks bail from Telangana high court in Himanta Sarma case
Article Text:
Image: IANS

HYDERABAD: Pawan Khera, AICC media and publicity department chairperson, has moved Telangana HC seeking anticipatory bail in a case registered by Guwahati police over his allegations against Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma 's wife. The plea will be heard on Thursday. Khera was booked under various BNS sections, including false information, forgery, cheating, criminal intimidation and conspiracy. In his April 7 petition, he said he has not been furnished a copy of the FIR and the allegations do not constitute a valid case.Khera had claimed that Sarma's wife holds foreign passports and has properties abroad not declared in his affidavit. Assam Police visited his Delhi residence and have now reached Hyderabad, seeking Telangana Police's help in tracing him.
Article Summary: Pawan Khera, AICC media and publicity department chairperson, has moved Telangana HC seeking anticipatory bail in a case registered by Guwahati police over his allegations against Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma's wife. The plea will be heard on Thursday.

Article Title: Lashkar-E-Taiba: 2 Pakistan LeT ultras, Jammu & Kashmir aide remanded in 10-day police custody
Article Text:
Image: IANS

SRINAGAR: The court of additional sessions judge, Srinagar, designated as a special court under the NIA Act, Tuesday remanded two Pakistani Lashkar-e-Taiba (LeT) terrorists and their Kashmiri accomplice in 10-day police custody, citing the gravity of their offences.Police arrested the trio — Pakistani LeT ultras Abdullah alias Abu Hureira and Khubaib alias Usman Bahi, and their associate Ghulam Mohammad Mir alias Mama from Kashmir — on April 6 in raids across J&K, Rajasthan and Haryana as part of an investigation that busted an interstate Lashkar-e-Taiba module.A senior police officer said Abdullah had infiltrated India about 16 years ago and remained active in Kashmir Valley. Mir was accused of providing logistical support, including shelter and food.The accused were booked under Sections 23, 18, 19, 20, 38 and 39 of Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act, and under Arms Act, including Section 25(1A) which prescribes stringent punishment for possession, manufacture or transfer of prohibited arms or ammunition and under Explosive Substances Act for offences relating to using or conspiring to use explosive substances to cause harm.The accused were allegedly involved in grave offences that posed a threat to the security, sovereignty and integrity of India, the court noted, holding that sufficient grounds existed for their police custody since the probe was ongoing.
Article Summary: Police arrested the trio — Pakistani LeT ultras Abdullah alias Abu Hureira and Khubaib alias Usman Bahi, and their associate Ghulam Mohammad Mir alias Mama from Kashmir. The accused were booked under Sections 23, 18, 19, 20, 38 and 39 of Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act.

Article Title: Kerala Student Missing: After techie, another Kerala student goes missing at Karnataka's Chandradrona hills
Article Text:
,





CHIKKAMAGALURU: District police launched a search for a 16-year-old girl from Kerala who went missing in Chikkamagaluru’s Chandradrona Hills Tuesday.The Class 10 student, Srinandhanabecame untraceable during a trip to the hills with her family.Chikkamagaluru SP Jitendra Kumar Dayama said teams deployed thermal drones, and were scanning CCTV footage. They are examining call records of the girl and her parents.The forest, and fire and emergency personnel have joined the search. The operation, halted due to darkness, resumed Wednesday with additional SP Jayakumar joining the efforts.“We have intensified the search with multiple teams on the ground. Challenging terrain and dense mist are hampering operations,” a senior officer said.The incident comes days after a similar incident in Kodagu, where Kerala-based techie GS Sharanya went missing during a trek to Tadiandamol. She was found about 72 hours later.Forest minister Eshwar Khandre, meanwhile, assured full departmental support to trace the girl.
Article Summary: The Class 10 student, Srinandhanabecame untraceable during a trip to the hills with her family. The forest, and fire and emergency personnel have joined the search. The incident comes days after a similar incident in Kodagu, where Kerala-based techie GS Sharanya went missing.

Article Title: M K Stalin: MK Stalin slams transfer of Tamil Nadu officials, calls EC ‘a branch of BJP’
Article Text:
Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M.K. Stalin accused the Election Commission of acting as a 'branch of BJP' after it transferred key state officials, including the Chief Secretary and DGP (DVAC).





TIRUVANNAMALAI: Hours after Election Commission (EC) transferred TN chief secretary N Muruganandam and DGP (DVAC) S David Devasirvatham on Wednesday, chief minister M K Stalin accused the commission of acting like “a branch of BJP .”Earlier in the day, EC replaced Muruganandam with senior bureaucrat M Sai Kumar and Devasirvatham with Sandeep Mittal, a 1995-batch IPS officer. Questioning EC’s action, Stalin said nobody can stop DMK ’s victory through such moves.“Who are you trying to help by transferring the officials,’’ Stalin asked during his campaign at Tiruvannamalai and Kallakurichi districts on Wednesday.“Just a few days ago, EC officials visited Chennai and stated that the law and order situation in TN was excellent and the administration was fully cooperative. What changed in a week,” Stalin asked. He alleged that these transfers, particularly in places such as Salem, were orchestrated to benefit AIADMK general secretary Edappadi K Palaniswami and the NDA coalition. Salem collector Brindha Ravi was transferred a day ago and Arun Thamburaj was posted in her place. “Is it happening to help EPS,’’ he asked.Stalin also questioned whether EC transferred the officials in a similar manner in BJP-ruled states ahead of polls. “Were the chief secretary and DGP transferred like this during the Bihar elections? This is a blatant attempt to stall the DMK’s victory, but they will fail miserably,” he said.Stalin slammed Palaniswami for being a “silent partner” in BJP’s alleged anti-Tamil agenda.He criticised Palaniswami for supporting the rapid and “stressful” special intensive revision (SIR) of voter lists, which DMK had opposed to protect voters’ rights and reduce the burden on govt staff.“Mr Palaniswami, tell us honestly. Have you ever stood by the people of Tamil Nadu against your Delhi owners? Have you ever condemned a single act of BJP that goes against the Tamil language or our rights,” Stalin asked. He warned that an NDA victory would mean the immediate termination of welfare schemes such as Vidiyal Payanam (free bus travel) and Urimai Thogai (rights grant).
Article Summary: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M.K. Stalin accused the Election Commission of acting as a 'branch of BJP' after it transferred key state officials. He alleged that these transfers were orchestrated to benefit AIADMK general secretary Edappadi K Palaniswami and the NDA coalition.

Article Title: 71-year-old man ‘rapes’ 7-year-old girl after luring her into shop in Andhra Pradesh
Article Text:
(The victim's identity has not been revealed to protect her privacy as per Supreme court directives on cases related to sexual assault)





RAJAHMUNDRY: A 71-year-old man allegedly raped a seven-year-old girl at his grocery store after luring her with chocolates and biscuits, on Tuesday afternoon at Achanta town in West Godavari district.According to the Achanta police, accused Velagan Venkata Swamy, a widower who runs a mom-and-pop store in the neighbourhood, resorted to the heinous act when the girl went to his shop to buy chocolates.As there was no one around, he allegedly lured her into his shop on the pretext of giving her more chocolates and biscuits. She followed him into the shop.Accused Swamy, who lives in the neighbourhood with his son’s family, sexually assaulted the girl, ignoring her cries, police said.After returning home, the girl, writhing in pain, told her mother about the incident.The mother lodged a complaint with the Achanta police the same night, and police sent the girl to the Palakollu govt hospital for a medical examination on Wednesday morning.Police have registered a case and formed teams to nab the accused who is currently at large. Achanta police sub-inspector Venkata Ramana said the girl’s parents hail from a lower economic strata and run a small fruit shop in the neighbourhood.
Article Summary: The victim's identity has not been revealed to protect her privacy as per Supreme court directives on sexual assault. Velagan Venkata Swamy, a widower who runs a mom-and-pop store in the neighbourhood, resorted to the heinous act when the girl went to his shop.
